title: Microsoft Q3 2026 Results: Cloud and AI Drive Strong Performance
text: Microsoft reported robust third-quarter results for the period ending March 31, 2026, demonstrating the strength of its cloud and AI initiatives. Revenue reached $82.9 billion, representing 18% year-over-year growth. Operating income climbed to $38.4 billion, up 20% annually. Net income totaled $31.8 billion, increasing 23% on a GAAP basis. Diluted earnings per share hit $4.27, up 23%. Chief Executive Satya Nadella highlighted the exceptional performance of Microsoft's artificial intelligence segment. He stated: Our AI business surpassed an annual revenue run rate of $37 billion, up 123% year-over-year. This dramatic expansion underscores the company's strategic focus on AI infrastructure and solutions. Microsoft Cloud generated $54.5 billion in revenue with 29% growth, while commercial remaining performance obligations nearly doubled to $627 billion. Productivity and Business Processes comprising Microsoft 365, LinkedIn, and Dynamics achieved $35 billion in revenue, growing 17%. Intelligent Cloud revenue reached $34.7 billion with 30% expansion, driven by Azure's 40% growth rate. More Personal Computing experienced modest decline at $13.2 billion, down 1%. Microsoft distributed $10.2 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases during the quarter.
